channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How Pittsburgh — host of this year's NFL draft — became a sports mecca
item.description: The Pennsylvania city is hosting the draft for the first time in almost 80 years. Pittsburghers say the city's passionate fanbases and winning teams make the selection a natural fit.
item.content:encoded:
The Pennsylvania city is hosting the draft for the first time in almost 80 years. Pittsburghers say the city's passionate fanbases and winning teams make the selection a natural fit.

(Image credit: Jeff Swensen for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is extended by 3 weeks as tensions rise in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hezbollah and Israel traded fire just hours after the ceasefire extension was announced, underscoring its fragility.
item.content:encoded:
Hezbollah and Israel traded fire just hours after the ceasefire extension was announced, underscoring its fragility.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.
item.content:encoded:
Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: 'Self-aware' robots can learn complex tasks by watching humans. Is that a good thing?
item.description: Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions about how much risk comes with letting robots be in charge of their own learning.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions about how much risk comes with letting robots be in charge of their own learning. 

(Image credit: Malte Mueller)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: After 2 failed votes, Mike Johnson unveils new plan to extend key U.S. spy powers
item.description: With an April 30 deadline fast approaching, Johnson unveiled his latest proposal to extend the controversial surveillance program known as FISA 702.
item.content:encoded:
With an April 30 deadline fast approaching, Johnson unveiled his latest proposal to extend the controversial surveillance program known as FISA 702.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Why Trump wants to spend $1 billion on Great Salt Lake
item.description: Utah's Great Salt Lake has been labeled an "environmental nuclear bomb" and it has the attention of the president of the United States.
item.content:encoded:
Utah's Great Salt Lake has been labeled an "environmental nuclear bomb" and it has the attention of the president of the United States.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Ongoing U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz strands thousands of seafarers, Trump administration eases rules on medical marijuana, Wildfires fueled by drought continue to spread in parts of Georgia.
item.content:encoded: Ongoing U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz strands thousands of seafarers, Trump administration eases rules on medical marijuana, Wildfires fueled by drought continue to spread in parts of Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: DHS starts deportation of woman whose loved one died on Baltimore's Key Bridge
item.description: The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.
item.content:encoded:
The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.

(Image credit: Wesley Lapointe for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Cuba's ambassador to Mexico says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
item.description: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, Cuba's ambassador to Mexico blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.
item.content:encoded: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, Cuba's ambassador to Mexico blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta
